Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; industry standard is 250 business days
Short Description: The number of historical trading days used to construct the return distribution for historical-simulation VaR. A longer lookback captures more market regimes; a shorter lookback is more responsive to recent volatility.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: 1 day / 10 days; defaults to 1 day for trading book
Short Description: The period over which the potential loss is measured. Regulatory VaR under Basel typically uses a 10-day holding period; internal trading VaR typically uses 1 day.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Historical Simulation / Parametric (Variance-Covariance) / Monte Carlo
Short Description: The calculation methodology used for VaR. Historical simulation replays actual P&L scenarios; parametric assumes normal return distribution; Monte Carlo generates synthetic price paths using modelled volatility and correlation.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; eg. Crude -30%, Gas +50%, 2008 Financial Crisis replay
Short Description: A user-defined label for a stress test scenario. Each scenario specifies price shocks applied to one or more commodity indices to test portfolio resilience under extreme but plausible conditions.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; expressed as a percentage change to the current index price
Short Description: The magnitude of the price shock applied to each commodity index under a given stress scenario. Eg. -30% shock to Brent crude. Applied to current MTM positions to calculate stressed P&L and exposure.

Derived From / Calculation: Net Position × (Index Price × (1 + Shock%) − Trade Price)
Short Description: The hypothetical portfolio P&L resulting from applying the stress scenario price shocks to all open positions. Compared against available capital and credit limits to assess tail risk.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all currencies defined in Master Data → Currency
Short Description: The currency in which the counterparty credit limit is expressed. Exposure in other currencies is converted to this currency using the FX rates defined in Master Data → Currency before comparing against the limit.

Derived From / Calculation: Current Exposure by Counterparty ÷ Credit Limit × 100
Short Description: The percentage of a counterparty's credit limit currently consumed by open trades. Displayed on the risk dashboard and used to trigger warning alerts (eg. at 80%) and hard breach flags (at 100%).

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; defaults to 80%
Short Description: The credit utilisation percentage at which a soft warning is surfaced to traders and risk managers. Reaching this threshold does not block trade capture but triggers a notification requiring acknowledgement.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; 20-character ISO 17442 code
Short Description: The globally unique 20-character Legal Entity Identifier assigned to the entity under the ISO 17442 standard. Mandatory for EMIR and MiFID II trade reporting where the entity is a reportable counterparty. Eg. 2138004WTZTCBQH8JV73.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; 8 or 11-character ISO 9362 code
Short Description: The Bank Identifier Code (BIC) assigned to the entity for interbank payment routing and SWIFT messaging. Required for settlement instruction matching and payment initiation. Eg. BARCGB22.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; expressed in Credit Limit Currency
Short Description: The maximum aggregate exposure the firm is willing to carry against this counterparty at any one time. Compared against Exposure by Counterparty in Risk and Analytics to determine utilisation and breach status.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all currencies defined in Master Data → Currency
Short Description: The currency in which the counterparty credit limit is expressed. All exposures in other currencies are converted to this currency using current FX rates before the breach check is performed.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Pending / Approved / Expired / Suspended
Short Description: Indicates the current Know Your Customer (KYC) and AML due diligence status for this counterparty. Trades may only be booked against counterparties with Approved KYC status. Expired status triggers a renewal workflow.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user or selection from calendar
Short Description: The date on which the counterparty's KYC documentation is next due for periodic review. A system alert is generated a defined number of days before this date to prompt the compliance team to initiate renewal.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Financial Counterparty (FC) / Non-Financial Counterparty (NFC) / NFC+ / Third-Country Entity
Short Description: The counterparty's regulatory classification under EMIR (EU) or equivalent regime. Classification as FC or NFC+ triggers mandatory clearing and margin obligations for OTC derivatives.
Unique, system-generated identifier assigned to a nomination record when it is first saved.
The deal number of the physical trade to which this nomination relates. One trade may have multiple nominations across different gas days or scheduling windows.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user or selection from calendar
Short Description: The specific calendar date for which the nominated volume is to be delivered or received. For gas markets this corresponds to the gas day (eg. 06:00–06:00).

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Day-Ahead / Intraday / Real-Time / Within-Day
Short Description: Indicates the market window in which the nomination is submitted. Different windows have different cut-off times and tolerance bands set by the pipeline or grid operator.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all TSOs / DSOs / pipeline operators defined in Master Data
Short Description: The transmission system operator, distribution system operator, or pipeline operator to whom the nomination is submitted. Eg. National Grid, Gasunie, Colonial Pipeline.
The volume the trader nominates to deliver or receive on the specified gas day and at the specified point. May differ from the contracted quantity within tolerance bands permitted by the operator.

Derived From / Calculation: Received from counterparty or pipeline operator confirmation
Short Description: The volume nominated by the counterparty for the same delivery point and day. A nomination is matched when both sides agree; a mismatch generates an imbalance.
The current status of the nomination as returned by the pipeline or grid operator. A Confirmed status means the operator has accepted the volume; Matched means both counterparty nominations agree.
The difference between the volume nominated and the volume actually delivered or received. Imbalances may attract operator-imposed charges or cashout prices and are tracked for settlement purposes.

Derived From / Calculation: Auto-populated when nomination is submitted to operator
Short Description: The date and time at which the nomination was submitted to the pipeline or grid operator. Used to confirm compliance with the operator's cut-off deadline for the scheduling window.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Commodity Invoice / Demurrage Invoice / Freight Invoice / Commission Invoice / Tax Invoice
Short Description: Classifies the invoice by its commercial purpose. Commodity invoices settle the physical delivery; demurrage invoices settle vessel delay charges; freight invoices settle transportation; commission invoices settle broker fees.

Derived From / Calculation: Invoice Amount × applicable Tax Rate defined in Master Data
Short Description: The tax or VAT amount applicable to the invoice, calculated by applying the relevant tax rate to the taxable base amount. The tax code and rate are derived from the counterparty's tax jurisdiction and the commodity type.

Derived From / Calculation: From counterparty jurisdiction and commodity type in Master Data
Short Description: The tax classification code applied to the invoice line, indicating the applicable VAT / GST / withholding tax rate and reporting category. Eg. S20 (Standard Rate 20%), Z0 (Zero-rated), EX (Exempt).
The reference number assigned to this invoice by the counterparty or external party, used for matching incoming payment advices and reconciling against counterparty records.

Derived From / Calculation: Checkbox / Pick list: Undisputed / Disputed / Under Review
Short Description: Indicates whether the invoice is subject to a commercial dispute raised by the counterparty. Disputed invoices are excluded from overdue calculations until resolved and trigger a workflow notification to the back office.

Derived From / Calculation: Free text; applicable only when Dispute Flag = Disputed or Under Review
Short Description: A free-text description of the reason the invoice is disputed by the counterparty. Eg. quantity discrepancy, pricing formula disagreement, or documentation deficiency.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; entered once the BL is issued at load port
Short Description: The date on which the bill of lading is issued at the load port, representing the moment title and/or risk transfers depending on the Incoterm. Used to determine the pricing period for floating-price cargoes and to trigger invoice generation.

Derived From / Calculation: Surveyor's figure entered by user once BL is issued
Short Description: The gross and net quantity of commodity certified on the bill of lading by an independent surveyor at load port. This is the contractual delivered quantity for invoice purposes and may differ from the nominated quantity.

Derived From / Calculation: Entered by user from independent surveyor's report at discharge port
Short Description: The quantity of commodity determined by draft survey at the discharge port. Where the contract specifies discharge-port quantity as the invoice basis, this figure overrides the BL quantity for settlement.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user when vessel tenders NOR at load or discharge port
Short Description: The date and time at which the vessel tenders Notice of Readiness to the terminal, signalling the vessel is in position and ready to load or discharge. NOR tendering triggers the commencement of laytime.

Derived From / Calculation: Calculated from NOR date/time adjusted for any free time or notice period per charter party terms
Short Description: The date and time at which laytime starts counting, after applying any contractual notice period or free time allowance to the NOR tendering time. This is the start point for demurrage calculations.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user from charter party / contract terms
Short Description: The total number of hours permitted under the contract or charter party for loading or discharging operations. Time used beyond this allowance incurs demurrage; time saved may attract despatch.

Derived From / Calculation: Derived from actual completion time minus laytime commencement
Short Description: The actual number of hours elapsed from laytime commencement to completion of loading or discharging operations, as recorded in the Statement of Facts. Compared against Allowed Laytime to determine demurrage or despatch.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user from charter party; expressed as currency per day (eg. USD/day)
Short Description: The contractual daily rate payable to the shipowner when loading or discharging exceeds the allowed laytime. Expressed in the charter party currency per day, pro-rated for part-days.

Derived From / Calculation: If Time Used > Allowed: (Time Used − Allowed) × Demurrage Rate. If Time Used < Allowed: (Allowed − Time Used) × Despatch Rate
Short Description: The calculated demurrage payable to the vessel owner (time used exceeds allowed laytime) or despatch receivable from the vessel owner (time saved). This amount is invoiced separately from the commodity invoice.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user from freight contract or spot fixture
Short Description: The total freight charge payable for transporting the cargo, either as a lump sum (eg. USD 500,000) or on a per-unit basis (eg. USD 8.50/MT). Captured separately from the commodity price for P&L attribution.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Lump Sum / Per MT / Per BBL / Per MMBTU
Short Description: Indicates whether the freight charge is expressed as a total lump sum for the voyage or as a per-unit rate applied to the BL quantity. Determines how the freight cost is calculated and allocated.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user or updated from vessel-tracking feed
Short Description: The estimated time of arrival of the vessel at the load or discharge port. Used for operational planning, terminal scheduling, and tracking whether the vessel is on laycan.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user from contract terms; selection from calendar
Short Description: The first date of the laytime cancelling window — the earliest date on which the vessel may arrive and tender NOR without being rejected. If the vessel arrives before laycan start, the charterer may reject it.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user from contract terms; selection from calendar
Short Description: The last date of the laytime cancelling window. If the vessel fails to tender NOR by this date, the charterer has the right to cancel the voyage without liability.
Short Description
Unique, system-generated identifier assigned to a broker record when it is first saved in the system.
The full legal or trading name of the brokerage firm. Eg. ICAP, Marex, Tradition. This is the entity that intermediated the trade and to whom commission is payable.
An abbreviated identifier for the broker used in blotter views and reports. Up to 10 characters. Eg. ICAP, MRX, TRAD.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Voice Broker / Electronic Broker / Hybrid
Short Description: Indicates whether trades are brokered via telephone (voice), via an electronic trading platform (eg. ICE, Tradition Trayport), or a combination of both.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Active / Suspended / Do Not Use
Short Description: The current operational status of the broker. Only brokers with Active status may be selected on new trade capture screens.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all Broker Fee definitions in Master Data → Broker Commissions
Short Description: The default commission schedule applied when this broker is selected on a trade. Can be overridden at trade level. Links to the Broker Commission definition which holds rate, unit, and currency.

Indicates whether the broker is a regulated entity under the applicable jurisdiction (eg. FCA-authorised in the UK, CFTC-registered in the US). Relevant for EMIR / Dodd-Frank transaction reporting obligations.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; 20-character ISO 17442 code
Short Description: The globally unique 20-character Legal Entity Identifier assigned to the broker under ISO 17442. Required for regulatory trade reporting under EMIR, MiFID II, and Dodd-Frank where the broker is a reportable counterparty.

Derived From / Calculation: Auto populated when Trade is captured in the system
Short Description: Unique identifier for a trade that doesn't change throughout the life of the trade. Eg. 205637

Identifies the version of the trade, the transaction number and deal number are same unless the trade has been modified. Modified trades are assigned a new transaction number but keep the original deal number.
Shared number for trades using common Instrument, and always unique in case of over the counter (OTC) trades

Derived From / Calculation: Auto populated when Trade is captured in the system
Short Description: The trade input timestamp in the system
The date the trade was made on. A date may be entered manually or by selecting the date from the calendar

Derived From / Calculation: Based on selection from the user on Trade input screen
Short Description: Indicates whether the trader's role in the trade is as a buyer or seller. Pay/Receive is derived based on Buy/Sell selection
Identifies whether the physical/financial side will be delivered/received or received/paid. This is derived from Buy/Sell

Derived From / Calculation: Derived from user's selection in the drop-down menu while processing a trade
Short Description: It is the current status of the trade, which is initially set to Pending, when a trade is processed the status moves to which the trade was processed to. Eg. Validated, New

Derived From / Calculation: Manually input by the user or selection from the calendar
Short Description: Start date of the physical/financial side of the deal

Derived From / Calculation: Manually input by the user or selection from the calendar
Short Description: End date of the physical/financial side of the deal
The time the day starts. Eg. Gas day starts at 6:00am

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the internal parties defined in the system
Short Description: This is the internal party / business unit involved in a trade, can be selected from the pick list which shows all the parties defined in the system

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the internal legal entities defined in the system
Short Description: The legal entity associated with the selected business unit

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the personnels / users defined in the system
Short Description: The portfolio associated with the selected business unit

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the internal portfolios defined in the system

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the external business units / counterparty units defined in the system

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the products/commodities defined in the system
Short Description: The product/commodity being traded
The amount of product/commodity that is to delivered

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all volume types defined in the system
Short Description: Indicates if the volume is on a contract, daily, hourly, period or yearly basis. For eg. If the deal notional is 1000, and the deal volume type is hourly, then the volume would be calculated as 1000*24 hours

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the units defined in the system, usually it is unit conversion table
Short Description: The unit in which the volume is quoted. Eg. MT for refined products, MMBTU for Natural Gas, BBL for Oil
To identify whether the price is fixed or Index/Formula, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the index / curves defined in the system
Short Description: The index or curve used to determine the projected value of future flows of a product/commodity & payments. This applies if valuation is based upon a market index
The fixed price or the spread off a projected index price
The currency in which payments are made, defaults from the currency entered in the index/curve
The transportation terms which indicates the buyer and sellers responsibility in the business transaction. Eg. CIF, CFR
The location where the commodity will be loaded at
The location where the commodity will be discharged at
Query in the comments
This is a calculated field and I believe shouldn't be part of this category, we can add it in a new category for calculated fields such as in PnL reports
This is a calculated field and I believe shouldn't be part of this category, we can add it in a new category for calculated fields such as in PnL reports

The period of time or frequency between payments
Shifts the payment period a specified number of good business days from each payment date defined by the settlement period

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the transport classes defined in the system
Short Description: The mode of transportation that is used to move the commodity. Eg. Barge, Pipeline, Rail, Truck, TBD, etc.

From FX Trade or FX Market

Date that identifies the date and time when it was last modified
Number that identifies the no. of times the record was modified

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the portfolios defined in the system
Short Description: Indicates the portfolio that has been assigned as the default for the BU
Free text field to enter address
Free text field to enter City
Free text field to enter State
Free text field to enter Country
Free text field to enter Phone
Each client determines the specific use of this field, allows them to individually define certain fields as and how they want
Unique ID generated and assigned to a personell record when it was entered in the system
A variable charcter up to 32 characters that uniquely identifies the person that was added to the personnel list
Text field to enter user's first name
Text field to enter user's last name
Text field to enter user's phone
Text field to enter user's email
Text field to enter user's address
Text field to enter user's city
Text field to enter user's state
Text field to enter user's country
Identifies whether the personnel is Internal, External or a Licensed User
Status of the record. Eg. Auth Pending, Authorized, Do Not Use
Indicates what license to which the user is entitiled. Eg. Full Access, Server, Read Only, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Auto populated when a fee type is entered in the system
Short Description: Unique identification for the fee type
Name of the fee type input by the user

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the commission types defined in the system
Short Description: The type of broker commission. Eg. Clearing, Execution, OTC, OTC Clearing, Service
The defined rate for the broker commission
To define whether the commission is default
The exchange rate defined for the broker commission

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the brokerage units defined in the system
Short Description: Unit of broker commission. Eg. Per Avg Notional, Per Trade, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the currencies defined in the system
Short Description: The currency in which the broker commission is paid
The frequency in which the broker commission is paid. Eg. Monthly, Quarterly
The minimum fee attached to the commission
Tha maximum fee attached to the commission

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list items are derived from all the index groups defined in the system in the static data
Short Description: Used to narrow down the available broker fees based on Index group. It is common for a broker to charge different rates based on the commodity, eg. $2/lot for NatGas and $3/lot for Oil
Email, phone
Yes/No

Derived From / Calculation: Auto-populated when a index definition is saved in the system
Short Description: System generated unique identification number for the index once it is saved in the system

Derived From / Calculation: Auto-populated when a index definition is saved in the system
Short Description: This number changes each time a modification is made to the index
Name of the index according to the convention. Eg. Fixed income curves must be named as INDEX NAME.Currency i.e. LIBOR.USD

Derived From / Calculation: Derived from user's selection in the drop-down menu while processing a Index
Short Description: Status of the index such as Validated, Pending, New, etc.
Status of the index - Custom (used for pricing, hedging and running simulations), Official (used for end to end portfolio revaluation), Template (allow users to recall as a base for a new curve)
User defined text field for label identification. Eg. This could be used to defined a trading region (eg. West)

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing the different market and specific curve types defined in the system
Short Description: Defines the market of which this index is a measure. Eg. Commodities, Currencies, Equities, Spot Price, Fixed Income

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing the different underlying product groups defined in the system
Short Description: Further defines the market, depends on market selected in the market field, choices include Swap, Govt and Repo. If the market is commodities, choices include Precious Metal, Electricity, Natural Gas, Crude Oil, etc.
Further defines the group. If the choice is NatGas then the available options could be Gas Physical EU, Gas Physical UK, Gas Capacity EU, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing various formats of indexes available within the system
Short Description: Determines the type of output this index produces. Eg. Price, DF, Avg Rate, Ratio, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the index definition classes defined in the system
Short Description: It specifies what each grid point interval represents. Eg. Date-base Fwd, Time-base Fwd, 2-D Grid, External Model, etc.
It defines the delivery unit associated with the index, if the index is interest rate or currency, choose Currency. Otherwise, select the relevant commodity measure (eg. MWh for Power)
A sequence of dates generated by formulas as defined by system users. It usually controls rate reset and pricing periods for instruments that use this index. Eg. FOM, FOY, icebrent, etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing the payment conventions available in the system
Short Description: Identifies how the payment dates are modified for instruments using this index

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user or selection from calendar
Short Description: The end date for the index. Eg. 1y or 60y, grid points that exceed the coverage date are deactivated

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing all the interpolation methods defined In the system
Short Description: The method used to calculate prices between grid points. Eg. Back-Step, Front-Step, Linear
Applicable to child index, to choose whether delta sensitivity should be calculated for the child and parent or only child

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing all the discounting indexes in the system
Short Description: Discount indexes available for use as default discounting index for instruments/trade using this index

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing all the ref source defined in the system
Short Description: The source of the pricing data

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the methods defined in the system
Short Description: The logic used in calculating future payments which are unknown (floating) at the time of trade entry
Hour start time (0:00, 12:00, 06:00) etc.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the holiday schedules defined in the system
Short Description: Defines the holiday schedule to be applied to the index. Eg. NYMEX, ICE UK, LONDON

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the currencies defined in the system
Short Description: Defines the base currency for the index
Defines whether the index is composite (derived from 1 or more indexes based on some formula and used in case of missing prices for a given market) or standard (single market view - prices, rates)
System generated based on when the index was last updated by the user

Derived From / Calculation: Sytsem generated once a grid point is entered and saved
Short Description: Unique ID for each grid point indicating its order in the timeline
Label name for the grid point

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all the instruments defined in the static data
Short Description: Allows users to categorize grid points based on the instruments they represent. Eg. Swap, Forward-D, Forward-M, Futures-D, Futures-M

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list containing 8 priority levels - one is the highest and eight is the lowest priority
Short Description: Determines which grid point receives precedence when the dates of two or more grid points overlap

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user, can be selected from the calendar
Short Description: A fixed start date or relative start date such as Current date, next gbd, etc. Can be either a symbolic date or an absolute date

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user, can be selected from the calendar
Short Description: A fixed or relative end date such as 1m (one month after the start date)
Start time, only enabled if the curve class is 'Time-base Fwd"
Start time, only enabled if the curve class is 'Time-base Fwd"
Specifies the value used to calculate the delta result for the grid point
Controls how delta senstivity is calculated i.e., effective, raw, no

Subtab: Product UOM / Pricing UOM / Pricing Unit

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all instrument types defined in the system
Short Description: Identifies whether the financial instrument is a Swap, Future, Forward, Option, or Swaption. Drives which additional fields are activated on the trade capture screen.
Indicates whether the financial trade settles by cash payment against an index or by physical delivery of the underlying commodity.

Derived From / Calculation: Derived from Exchange / Instrument definition in Master Data
Short Description: The standardised contract size for exchange-traded instruments. Eg. 1,000 BBL per lot for ICE Brent futures. Trade notional = Lot Size × Number of Lots.
The number of standardised contracts being traded. Used in conjunction with Lot Size to derive total notional volume for exchange-traded futures and options.

Derived From / Calculation: Lot Size × Number of Lots (for exchange-traded); or direct user input for OTC
Short Description: The total volume of the underlying commodity referenced by the financial instrument. For OTC instruments this is entered directly; for listed instruments it is derived from Lot Size × Lots.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; applicable only when Instrument Type = Option or Swaption
Short Description: The agreed price at which the option buyer may buy (call) or sell (put) the underlying commodity. Only enabled when Instrument Type is Option or Swaption.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Call / Put; applicable only when Instrument Type = Option or Swaption
Short Description: Indicates whether the option confers the right to buy (Call) or sell (Put) the underlying commodity at the Strike Price.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user; applicable only when Instrument Type = Option or Swaption
Short Description: The upfront premium paid by the option buyer to the option seller. Expressed per unit of the underlying commodity (eg. $/BBL). Generates a separate cash-flow leg on the settlement schedule.

Derived From / Calculation: Input by the user or selection from calendar; applicable only when Instrument Type = Option or Swaption
Short Description: The last date on which the option may be exercised. For European-style options this is also the only exercise date.
Defines when the option may be exercised. American = any time before expiry; European = on expiry date only; Asian = payoff based on average price over a period.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all exchanges and CCPs defined in Master Data → Exchange
Short Description: The exchange or central counterparty clearing house (CCP) through which the trade is cleared. Eg. ICE Clear Europe, CME Clearing. Applicable to exchange-traded and cleared OTC instruments.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all counterparties with clearing-house flag enabled in Master Data
Short Description: The futures commission merchant (FCM) or clearing member through whom the firm's trades are cleared and margin is posted with the CCP.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list of all accounts defined in Master Data → Accounts with account type = Margin
Short Description: The account held at the clearing broker against which initial margin and variation margin calls are posted and collected.

Derived From / Calculation: Derived from clearing house SPAN/TIMS margin model applied to open position
Short Description: The upfront collateral required by the CCP when a new futures or cleared swap position is opened. Recalculated daily based on position size and implied volatility.

Derived From / Calculation: Daily MTM gain or loss on the open position: (Settlement Price − Previous Settlement Price) × Notional
Short Description: The daily cash settlement of unrealised profit or loss on an open futures or cleared swap position. Positive variation margin is received; negative is paid to the clearing house.

Derived From / Calculation: Pick list: Fair Value Hedge / Cash Flow Hedge / Economic Hedge / Speculative
Short Description: Indicates whether the financial trade is designated as a qualifying hedge under IFRS 9 / ASC 815 and, if so, the hedge type. Drives accounting treatment of MTM movements.

Derived From / Calculation: Free text or pick list linking to a physical Trade ID or portfolio
Short Description: Identifies the physical trade, contract, or forecast transaction being hedged by this financial instrument. Required for hedge accounting designation under IFRS 9 / ASC 815.
